entries.change_summary: Add a note reflecting the FMCSA final rule (FR doc #2026-03256, eff. 2026-03-23) that creates a limited exception to the all-wheels braking requirement for pre-2010 portable conveyors used in aggregate industry operations, amending 49 CFR Part 393. The Federal Register document 2026-12448 (effective 2026-07-22) rescinds only the requirement that a physical copy of the ELD operator's manual be kept in the commercial motor vehicle — a narrow administrative/paperwork obligation under 49 CFR Part 395. The published post does not mention, rely upon, or implicitly assume the existence of that operator's manual requirement anywhere in its text. The post's regulatory claims concern ELD data integrity, unassigned driving segments, edit histories, personal conveyance annotation, velocity-distance correlation, cross-document verification, and the falsification violation standard under 49 CFR 395.8(e) and 395.15 — none of which are affected by the rescission of the in-cab manual requirement. Because the post contains no assertion that is rendered inaccurate or incomplete by this specific regulatory change, no update is warranted.
